ads.author: Ron Bennett
ads.title: Defender Arcade Williams
ads.description: Defender Arcade. I have had this for many years. This game didn't work when i got, basically because someone had taken the boards and power supply from it. I bought a set of working boards and a switching power supply from Arcadeshop. All was well and working as it should until the power supply went out a couple years later. There it sat until i got a new power supply, but then the board had an issue, so i sent the boards out to have them checked out and repaired. It worked for a while again, then i had a board issue again, and sent them out again for a look and repair, the main board needed a chip replaced. The boards are still in the box, i haven't gotten around to checking it out. I have new T-molding for it. The original back door didn't come with the game when i got it, i cut a piece of plywood for the back. It also doesn't have the service switch, i was also gone when i got the game. The front artwork i re-did with stencils, the side artwork i didn't do, because i had an issue with the stencils, so the sides are just black. I was going to buy new side artwork, But, again, other things to do.

ads.author: Ron Bennett
ads.title: Revolution X Arcade Aerosmith Themed
ads.description: I have had this game for many years. It is not currently working. As soon as the cord is plugged in, it blows the fuse ( with the on/off switch in either position ) Worked great until this began including both sound and screen picture. I was going to unplug everything that comes from the power supply, plug the power cord in, then begin plugging things back into the power supply one at a time until the fuse blew to see what exactly is causing the fuse to blow, but i have too many other things going on.

ads.author: Kat Ann
ads.title: Namco Crisis Zone
ads.description: FOR SALE Namco Crisis Zone The game powers up but nothing comes on the screen. It worked fine one day, then didn't. We're not monitor experts and have no experience working on monitors. Need it gone. Will not ship. Located in Evans, GA (30809). It's big and heavy. Easy in and out of building for loading. $1,500 Here's a summary of the game from AI. Crisis Zone (1999) is an intense, action-packed arcade light-gun shooter by Namco and a spin-off of the Time Crisis series. Players control Claude McGarren, an elite STF agent, utilizing a mounted submachine gun (Steyr Mannlicher TMP) and a foot pedal system to take cover, reload, and destroy massive, highly destructible environments while battling terrorists in a 3D urban setting. Key Features and Gameplay: Unique Controller: Instead of a pistol, players use a mounted submachine gun with 40-round capacity and a laser sight. Cover Mechanism: A foot pedal allows players to take cover, acting as both a reload function and a shield against enemy fire. Destructible Environments: Players can destroy the environment, including shooting CDs off shelves, breaking glass, and destroying clothing racks. Campaign Structure: The game features three main stages (Subway, Hotel, Commercial Zone) that can be played in any order, followed by a final boss stage. Action-Oriented: The game is known for high-intensity, rapid-fire gameplay, often featuring faster enemy encounters than the standard Time Crisis. Crisis Zone is powered by Namco System 23 hardware and typically features 27-inch CRT or 50-inch projection displays.

It comes will all the parts, and a rebuild kit with new ball. The Marvel Arcade Cabinet needs a new controller deck cut and graphics. It has new speakers, wiring harness, controllers, and game board with over 800 games. The power board was reworked into a PC plug and switch. The CRT is in good shape and original to the cabinet as far as I know. It has holes from a lockout bracket above and below the coin door. Selling As-Is with new/extra parts. Nothing is really wrong with them that I’m aware, just never get around to finishing them and taking up space. $800 for both $700 for Totem Pinball $300 for the Arcade Machine

Everything works great including the blue lights which you can turn on and off independently. Police Trainer is a target shooting simulation game that takes 1 or 2 players through a series of levels created to help develop skills such as accuracy, speed, decision making, etc., as you compete for high scores. This game has been a lot of fun for our family and friends. It is all target shooting so there's no blood or actual violence; rated for mature responsible ages and individuals. This arcade machine is big and very heavy. If you need it delivered, text me and we might be able to work it out depending on delivery location. Dimensions: H=80in, D=40in, W=32in Asking $1500 but will entertain REASONABLE offers. If you lowball, I won't respond. If you have something to trade, text me and let me know. eight four three five one four nine nine seven five

All screws and cable ties are still in place. Very clean, all original. No mold, no smell, no cigarette burns. Missing the monitor, marquee, foot rest, and some of the joystick handle pieces. Does have the back door, coin box with lid, owners manual and all service and schematics. Overlay is great, side art is good. Unable to test as the monitor is missing. $700 bucks Thanks Also... located in Chattanooga, available for pickup anytime or can help with shipping.

Condition Notes: Machine powers on and lights up Ball launch works Flippers are not functioning (likely a simple repair for a pinball enthusiast) Cosmetic wear consistent with age, but glass, playfield, and cabinet graphics are vibrant Super colorful Wild West theme with dancers and boots Full-size, perfect for a vintage game room, collector, or restoration project. Pickup in Sumter, SC bring help, it’s heavy. Will consider offers or bundle deals with other vintage items. Great candidate for a light restoration or use as a display piece as-is.

ads.author: Allen Robinson
ads.title: P.O.W. Prisoners of War, Upright Arcade, SNK 1988
ads.description: Vintage Upright Arcade – P.O.W. Prisoners of War (SNK, 1988) Price: $325 Own a piece of late-80s arcade history! This is an original P.O.W. – Prisoners of War upright cabinet from SNK, released in 1988. Known as a classic scrolling fighter / beat ’em up, players battle their way through enemy compounds after escaping captivity. It’s a gritty, action-packed title that helped define the genre and remains a nostalgic favorite for collectors. Condition: - Cabinet is very old and needs a lot of love. - Boards need work and likely new caps. - Monitor powers on but the image quality is poor. - Lights come on. - No sound currently. - This is a project cabinet — ideal for restoration enthusiasts. Highlights: - Rare SNK beat ’em up from the golden age of arcades. - Upright cabinet with authentic late-80s design. - Great candidate for restoration or display in a retro collection. Important: - Sold as-is. - Serious buyers only. No I don’t deliver but it can be picked up or shipped (arranged by buyer) in Powder Springs, GA.

ads.author: Allen Robinson
ads.title: Pole Position, Upright Arcade, Atari/Namco 1982
ads.description: Vintage Upright Arcade – Pole Position (Atari/Namco, November 1982) Price: $400 Step into racing history with this original Pole Position upright cabinet — one of the most iconic arcade racers ever made. Released in 1982 by Atari/Namco, Pole Position was a groundbreaking driving simulator that set the standard for racing games. It’s widely recognized as one of the Top 50 most historically important video games of all time. Condition: - Cabinet is very old and needs some love. - Boards need work and likely new caps. - Monitor does not power on. - Lights come on. - No sound currently. - This is a project cabinet — perfect for restoration enthusiasts or collectors. Highlights: - Classic upright racer with simulator-style gameplay. - Rare piece of arcade history from the golden age. - Great candidate for restoration or display in a collection. Important: - Price is negotiable, but only slightly. - Sold as-is. - Serious buyers only. No I don’t deliver but it can be picked up or shipped (arranged by buyer) in Powder Springs, GA.

ads.title: Classic 1981 Nintendo Donkey Kong Original Arcade Machine in Dedicated Cabinet TKG4-UP-US Blue
ads.description: Up for sale is an authentic piece of gaming history: an all original 1981 Nintendo Donkey Kong upright arcade machine. This is a dedicated cabinet featuring the iconic "Nintendo Blue" finish and original artwork. The machine has been recently brought back to life after years of storage. It is currently in fully playable condition and only needs a final dial-in of the H-hold coil on the monitor in order to be perfect, which I do not know how to do. All original parts with zero modifications or customizations other than bypassing the coin mechanism to allow free play (this can be reversed easily). Pickup in Concord, NC or I can arrange delivery for an extra fee after payment has been made. Cash, Cashapp, Paypal, Money Order or Cashier Check. I will also take trades on gold and silver.

Cash only please* I had planned on restoring this arcade machine but life got in the way. It powers on and works to an extent but it needs TLC to get it fully functional. The picture convergence needs help. The jet ski needs an air compressor to make the ride functional which is missing so I never could test it. There's also a piece of aluminum trim on the left side of the jet ski that was missing when I got it. The Sega Model 2 inside has been replaced so the old one is included. You will need to bring a trailer with a ramp to load it. Both pieces are on wheels so it's relatively easy to move but very heavy.

ads.author: Darian Sikar
ads.title: *** 1976 All American Bally “Freedom” Pinball Machine
ads.description: Selling my awesome 1976 Ballys Freedom Pinball game. Works great.. about 5 light bulbs need to be changed. Back glass has peeling and missing paint. ( can buy another for about $200 ) Just put on 4 brand new legs and levelers. Takes quarters 4 players Comes with American Flag Led sign. Back side is a little rough but still not bad for its age. Located in North Port

ads.author: Jacob Thompson
ads.title: Pinball Machine - Williams
ads.description: 1960’s Williams Electronics Pinball Machine The two “wheel value” slots only work on certain wheel value positions. The guy I bought it from said the contacts need cleaning. I bought it for my restaurant and decided not to use it because it didn’t match our looks and it was too loud. Thought about keeping it but would rather have the space. I’m asking what I gave. No I will not take less. Yes it’s available.

ads.author: Eric Fisher
ads.title: (PENDING) Williams F-14 TOMCAT pinball machine
ads.description: F14 Tomcat pinball project. I got this game with a mpu that had major battery acid damage. So all it needs is a new or used MPU. I used the mpu from my Earthshaker and made sure all lights, flashers, and everything else was working along with any broken rubbers. I don’t care to fix it and just want it gone. No I won’t take 1k for it. Delivery is possible in Florida

ads.author: Martin Maignan
ads.title: Midway's 1994 arcade classic, Cruis'n USA
ads.description: This arcade machine is working. It is in descent shape all around with some imperfections. The cabinet is solid but there is some minor cabinet damage and wear and tare. (See pictures and make your own judgement). The game board is Cruisin' USA. The monitor looks good. The steering and buttons are both responsive. This arcade is a BLAST! This would make a amazing present for any age, add to your man cave, or your game room.

ads.title: Why Shark arcade game
ads.description: Sky Shark arcade game, works fine. It's in storage, I don't have any other pictures. $250 firm

ads.author: Aaron Cadle
ads.title: Kung Fu Master arcade cabinet, working
ads.description: Data East "KUNG FUMASTER" arcade cabinet. The game is working fine, although to be a really nice piece for your basement arcade, would need some elbow grease. Video is a little fuzzy, I did not try to adjust it. Has the original CRT monitor, but looks like an updated power supply. A few scuffs and bruises on the cabinet like you would expect from a routed machine, that otherwise ready to plug in and play at your next game night. Asking $500 or best offer, will consider trades on your nice, working arcade games. Can't deliver, but will help you load. Must be picked up at my business between 9:30 and 4:00 p.m.: 7134 Regal ln Knoxville, TN 37918

ads.description: Blue Taito arcade machine cabinet converted to Kicker. Non working. Kicker PCB included. Will need new cabinet base. Good project cabinet. Has a Wells Gardner K4600 monitor.

ads.title: Taito Classics Arcade Bartop
ads.description: Taito Classics bartop for sale. All original. Everything works. monitor has no burn. controls and sound work. Cash on pick up.

ads.author: Jeff Cohen
ads.title: Rare Sit-Down Cruis'n World Arcade Game Machine for Sale!
ads.description: Relive the golden age of arcade racing with this world-famous classic 90s sit-down racing game. Perfect for your game room or man cave. Highlights: • Authentic Cruis'n World arcade experience • Highly sought-after Sit-Down Cabinet and Racing Seat • Force feedback steering wheel, and nostalgic Cruis'n World music • Linkable to a second cabinet to create a connected pair The game works and plays (sound, controls, etc.), but the monitor needs service. Please see included photos and video. Sold as-is. Pickup only in Midtown Miami / Wynwood, Florida. Serious inquiries only with appropriate vehicle for transport and ability to lift and load the arcade. Building equipped with freight elevator and flat loading bay (no mechanical lift). Arcade cabinet has small wheels. Yes "It is available" if it is still listed for sale.
